title: Minimal-access percutaneous and self-retracting surgical system
abstract: Disclosed herein are embodiments of a minimally-invasive surgical system and methods of use which can mimic a mosquito proboscis to efficiently penetrate tissue. The delivery system can utilize a plurality of modular strut instruments to create a working tissue canopy and apply any number of surgical actions to a target tissue.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A surgical system comprising: an outer flexible sheath; a first plurality of struts positioned within or attached to the outer flexible sheath, the first plurality of struts having sharp tips that together form a unified tissue dissector, the tissue dissector having an advanced position for penetrating into tissue and a retracted position in which the first plurality of struts are configured to retract tissue radially outwardly; and a second plurality of struts advanceable from within the outer flexible sheath, the second plurality of stmts being expandable radially when advanced from the outer flexible sheath to form a canopy around a surgical site; wherein at least some of the second plurality of struts comprise working instruments configured to perform a function at the surgical site. 2. The surgical system of Claim 1, wherein the second plurality of struts is configured to form a grasping claw when withdrawn into the outer sheath. 3. The surgical system of Claim 1 or Claim 2, further comprising a hub and relay to connect one or more stmts to a processor and human or robotic interface. 4. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-3, wherein the first plurality of stmts comprises a pair of labrums and an inner needle. 5. The surgical system of Claim 4, wherein the second plurality of struts comprise strut instruments interspersed with the pair of labrums and inner needle. 6. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-5, wherein the unified tissue dissector is configured to retract tissue as the outer sheath docks proximal to the surgical site. 7. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-6, wherein the second plurality of stmts are configured to advance from within the outer sheath as the tissue dissector is withdrawn into the outer sheath. 8. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-7, further comprising a camera or endoscope positionable within the outer flexible sheath. 9. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-8, wherein the working instruments comprise one or more a micrograsping forceps, a microscissors, a navigation actuator, a 

 microdissector, a cautery instrument, a suction device, a vessel clip, a ligation instrument, a drug delivery device, an ultrasonic or microdoppler flow probe, and a spectroscopy probe. 10. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-9, further comprising a power source connected to at least one of the working instruments. 1 1. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-10, wherein the first plurality of struts comprises a pair of legs and a needle. 12. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1 -1 1 , wherein at least a portion of the tissue dissector is configured to vibrate. 13. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-12, wherein the outer sheath is bendable and steerable. 14. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1 -13, wherein each of the working instruments is configured to longitudinally retract and extend within a lumen of one of the second plurality of struts. 15. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-14, wherein each of the second plurality of struts comprises a bend, the bend directing the struts from extending radially outward to extending radially inwards. 16. The surgical system of Claim 15, wherein the bend comprises a hinge configured to change angles. 17. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1 -16, wherein some of the second plurality of struts do not have working instrument. 18. The surgical system of any one of Claims 1-17, wherein the first plurality of struts are configured to cut and retract tissue radially outwards to facilitate advancement of the system through tissue. 19. A method of using the surgical system of any one of Claims 1-18, the method comprising: advancing the surgical system to a target location within a target tissue, the advancing comprising cutting through tissue with the tissue dissector to enter the target location; distally advancing the second plurality of struts through the outer sheath and into the target location, where the second plurality of stmts radially expand as they are 

 distally advanced out of the outer sheath to form the retraction canopy around the tareet tissue; and performing the function on the target tissue using the working instruments. 20. A multi-functional surgical system comprising: an outer sheath having a proximal end, a distal end, and a lumen extending between the proximal end and the distal end; an inner module located within the lumen of the outer sheath, the inner module comprising a plurality of lumens extending from a proximal end to a distal end of the inner module; at least one labium extending through at least one of the plurality of lumens of the inner module and configured to extend out the distal end of the outer sheath, wherein the at least one labium is configured to penetrate tissue and to move from a radially closed position to a radially open position, and wherein the at least one labium is configured to withdraw into the inner module; and a plurality of struts, each of the plurality of struts extending through one of the plurality of lumens of the inner module, wherein the plurality of struts are configured to radially expand as they are advanced distally from the distal end of the outer sheath to form a canopy working area and to circumferentially move tissue; wherein at least some of the plurality of stmts comprise a surgical end tip having a surgical function. 21. The multi-functional surgical system of Claim 20, wherein the outer sheath and the inner module are flexible. 22. The multi-functional surgical system of Claim 20 or Claim 21, further comprising a camera or endoscope extending through one of the plurality of lumens of the inner module. 23. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-22, further comprising a hub and relay connected to a processor with a human or robotic interface. 24. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-23, further comprising a power source connected to at least one of the surgical end tips. 25. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-24, wherein the plurality of stmts are configured to form a grasping claw while being withdrawn into the outer sheath. 26. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-25, wherein the at least one labrum comprises a pair of legs each having a cutting surface and a needle. 27. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-26, wherein the surgical end tips comprise one or more of a micrograsping forcep, a navigation actuator, a microscissors, a microdissector, a cautery instrument, a suction device, a vessel clip, a ligation instrument, a drug delivery device, an ultrasonic or microdoppler flow probe, and a spectroscopy probe. 28. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-27, wherein at least a portion of the at least one labrum is configured to vibrate. 29. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-28, wherein the system is bendable and steerable. 30. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-29, wherein each of the end tips is configured to longitudinally retract and extend within a lumen of one of the plurality of struts. 31. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-30, wherein each of the plurality of struts comprises a bend, the bend directing the strut from extending radially outward to extending radially inwards. 32. The multi-functional surgical system of Claim 31, wherein the bend comprises a hinge configured to change angles. 33. The multi-functional surgical system of any one of Claims 20-32, wherein some of the plurality of stmts do not have a surgical end tip. 34. A method of performing surgery comprising: advancing a multi-functional surgical system having an outer sheath to a target location with a target tissue, the advancing comprising cutting through tissue with at least one labrum of the surgical system to enter the target location, the at least one labrum extending through a lumen of the outer sheath and out a distal end of the outer sheath; 

 discally advancing a plurality of stmts through the outer sheath and into the target location, wherein the plurality of struts radially expand as they are distally advanced out of the outer sheath to form a canopy working area around the target tissue, and wherein at least some of the plurality of struts includes an end effector; and performing an action on the target tissue using at least one of the end effectors of the plurality of struts. 35. The method of Claim 34, wherein the performing the action comprises cutting, cauterizing, dissection, clipping, ligation, drug delivery, suction, removal, or grasping. 36. The method of Claim 34 or Claim 35, wherein the surgical system is controlled robotically. 37. The method of any one of Claims 34-36, further comprising retracting the at least one labrum into the outer sheath prior to or simultaneously while distally advancing the plurality of stmts. 38. The method of any one of Claims 34-37, further comprising withdrawing the plurality of struts after performing the action, wherein the withdrawing comprises radially compressing the plurality of struts to grasp the target tissue and translate the target tissue towards the outer sheath. 39. A surgical system comprising: an outer sheath ; a first plurality of stmts positioned within or attached to the outer sheath, the first plurality of stmts comprising sharp tips that form a unified tissue dissector, the unified tissue dissector configured to penetrate into tissue and radially open and close to cut and retract tissue radially outwardly to facilitate advancement of the surgical system through soft tissue; and a second plurality of stmts configured to be advanced from within the outer sheath, the second plurality of stmts expanding radially when distally advanced from the outer sheath to form a retraction canopy around a surgical target site; wherein at least some of the second plurality of struts comprise working instruments configured to perform a function at the surgical target site. 40. The surgical system of Claim 39, wherein the second plurality of struts is configured to form a grasping claw when withdrawn into the outer sheath. 41. The surgical system of Claim 39 or Claim 40, further comprising a hub and relay to connect one or more struts to a processor and human or robotic interface. 42. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-41, wherein the first plurality of struts comprises a pair of labrums and an inner needle. 43. The surgical system Claim 42, wherein the second plurality of struts comprise stmt instruments interspersed with the pair of labrums and inner needle. 44. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-43, wherein the unified tissue dissector is configured to retract tissue as the outer sheath docks proximal to the surgical site. 45. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-44, wherein the second plurality of stmts are configured to advance from within the outer sheath as the tissue dissector is withdrawn into the outer sheath. 46. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-45, further comprising a camera or endoscope positionable within the outer flexible sheath. 47. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-46, wherein the working instruments comprise one or more a micrograspmg forceps, a microscissors, a navigation actuator, a microdissector, a cautery instrument, a suction device, a vessel clip, a ligation instrument, a drug delivery device, an ultrasonic or microdoppler flow probe, and a spectroscopy probe. 48. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-47, further comprising a power source connected to at least one of the working instruments. 49. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-48, wherein the first plurality of struts comprises a pair of legs and a needle. 50. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-49, wherein at least a portion of the tissue dissector is configured to vibrate. 51. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-50, wherein the outer sheath is bendable and steerable. 52. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-51, wherein each of the working instruments is configured to longitudinally retract and extend within a lumen of one of the second plurality of stmts. 53. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-52, wherein each of the second plurality of struts comprises a bend, the bend directing the struts from extending radially outward to extending radially inwards. 54. The surgical system of Claim 53, wherein the bend comprises a hinge configured to change angles. 55. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-54, wherein some of the second plurality of struts do not have working instrument. 56. The surgical system of any one of Claims 39-55, wherein the first plurality of struts are configured to cut and retract tissue radially outwards to facilitate advancement of the system through tissue. 57. A method of using the surgical system of any one of Claims 39-56, the method comprising; advancing the surgical system to a target location within a target tissue, the advancing comprising cutting through tissue with the tissue dissector to enter the target location; distally advancing the second plurality of struts through the outer sheath and into the target location, where the second plurality of struts radially expand as they are distally advanced out of the outer sheath to form the retraction canopy around the target tissue; and performing the function on the target tissue using the working instruments. 58. An assembly for a surgical system, the assembly comprising; a first surgical device comprising at least one labium at a distal end configured to penetrate tissue and to move from a radially closed position to a radially open position; and a second surgical device comprising a plurality of struts, each of the plurality of struts being configured to radially expand as they are advanced distally to form a canopy working area, wherein at least some of the plurality of inner struts comprise an end tip having surgical functionality. 59. A method of using the assembly of Claim 58, the method comprising: advancing the first surgical device near to a target tissue to form a tunnel: 

 withdrawing the first surgical device through the tunnel; advancing the second surgical device through the tunnel; distally and radially extending the plurality of struts of the second surgicalo form the canopy working area; and performing a surgical operation with at least one of the end tips.
